mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which translates into immediate positive market sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of rising INDPRO, or the signal reverses direction during inflationary supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with a low policy rate forces expectations of aggressive future tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces positive forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for term premium changes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth, or the signal consistently underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark over rolling 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP growth rate falling below 1.0 percent signals severe demand weakness and recessionary conditions, prompting portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in > 50% of sub-1% GDP growth quarters, or the threshold effect disappears when adjusted for base effects.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm synchronized supply and labor market strength, driving immediate positive equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive 5-day returns when either series moves independently, or the interaction term shows negative predictive power in out-of-sample tests.
